Everyone deserves access to quality health care. We cannot address our nation’s care shortage without understanding the social determinants of health that impact outcomes. We must stay committed to expanding access for everyone and not restricting it. https://t.co/C9hBI0Mh0r
I was shocked to hear of the passing of Senator Lindsey Graham. I’m holding his family, his staff, and the people of South Carolina in my thoughts.
Wishing you and your family a safe and joyful Independence Day. 🇺🇸

Today, we celebrate 250 years of America’s independence. We honor the courage and hope that has always defined our nation, and we pay tribute to all who are fighting, serving, and sacrificing to keep that promise alive.
I am grateful that the Supreme Court reaffirmed the independence of our Federal Reserve Board. Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook earned her seat through decades of expertise and leadership.
Our nation's independent regulators need to be shielded from political attacks, and we must remain vigilant against further abuses of power by this administration.

The Supreme Court struck down a state law requiring permission to carry guns in private spaces like stores, restaurants, and hotels. This sets a dangerous standard that threatens public safety.

The American people deserve better. We will continue to fight for common-sense

Today in Ed&W Committee, I urged my colleagues to support my amendment to H.R. 5505 so no student is punished for actions they didn’t commit. Accountability belongs with those responsible. Families deserve justice, and we shouldn’t undermine a bipartisan bill that saves lives. https://t.co/hJEqI6XohV

I remain committed to advancing legislation that protects our children and prevents the next tragedy before it occurs. We owe it to every family touched by gun violence, and to every family we can still protect, to meet this moment with courage and conviction. https://t.co/uWNCO08uGH

I was proud to join my colleagues in signing on to an amicus brief in defense of mifepristone access. We've seen the life-threatening consequences of the Dobbs decision for families across our country. I'm standing on the side of protecting reproductive freedoms.
Today marks four years since SCOTUS overturned Roe v. Wade. Reproductive care is health care. Your personal decisions belong to you and no one else.

Today we honor Juneteenth and all who fought for our freedom. Their struggle reminds us that progress is earned, not given. The work isn’t done. We must keep pushing for true justice and equity for all. https://t.co/heFyd8pGd1

This Black Music Month, we honor the artists whose creativity and influence continue to shape our culture. Their music carries our history, uplifts our communities, and inspires future generations. https://t.co/ZhEE4vpwOH

The Trump Administration abruptly terminated more than 300 public safety grants across 37 states.

That’s why Democrats have long supported collaborative, evidence-based approaches to combat crime.

Since 2022, fewer people have been killed from gun violence and murders are down nationwide.

The reality is that crime prevention works.

But, there have been setbacks.

Proud to support this bipartisan effort to preserve our outdoor heritage and honor the volunteers who keep this incredible trail accessible for all.

The 288-mile Benton MacKaye Trail connects communities across Georgia, Tennessee, and North Carolina, welcomes more than 100,000 visitors, and is maintained by hundreds of dedicated volunteers. https://t.co/AAj3veS1O0

I honor my son, Jordan, and every victim by putting forth common-sense solutions that save lives. #EndGunViolence https://t.co/LpVeByt4xz

After a lifetime of hard work, every American deserves the https://t.co/p2sRkRfMCq

Today, we remember and honor the fallen. The sacrifices they made for our freedom can never be repaid, only remembered with profound gratitude. We hold their memory in our hearts. https://t.co/aQ3LNDZbbZ

Veterans are far more likely to die by firearm suicide than the average American, yet this bill removes safeguards from the background check system with no regard for their safety. I stand with my colleagues against this dangerous change. https://t.co/WkgxKsVeUd

There is no excuse for paying someone below minimum wage just because of a disability. Job seekers with disabilities deserve real pathways to independence, employment, and fair wages for their work. This issue is personal to me. Equal work should earn equal pay. https://t.co/VKCyg7Y4se

I’m a child of the Civil Rights Movement, and I know what attacks on voting rights and Black representation look like. We’ve seen this before. Efforts to silence Black voices and weaken voting protections are a direct attack on our democracy and our hard-fought progress. https://t.co/mNTZvGmutu

Once again in America, a faith community has been devastated by gun violence. My heart is with the victims of the senseless shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, their families, and all those across the country who are grieving what appears to be an anti-Muslim attack.

Today, we celebrate 61 years of Head Start and the more than 40 million children and families it has served, including right here in Georgia’s 6th District. Strong communities start with strong foundations, and Head Start makes that possible.

Equal Pay Day is a reminder of how far we have to go and why we won’t stop fighting. That’s why I join my @DemWomenCaucus colleagues in championing the Paycheck Fairness Act to ensure all women are paid fairly for all the work they do.

March 26 is Equal Pay Day, the point in 2026 when women finally catch up to what men earned in 2025. For the second straight year, the pay gap has widened. Women working full-time earn just 81 cents for every dollar men earn and even less for many women of color. https://t.co/YbAsruPz24
